sections.title: DB Migrations
sections.current_value: Migrations run automatically on deploy via migrate-bot. Destructive migrations require a backup snapshot first. Max migration duration: 5 minutes.

sections.title: Feature Flags
sections.current_value: Flags are managed in LaunchDarkly. Stale flags are removed after 90 days. Every flag needs an owner and an expiry date.

sections.title: CI Pipeline
sections.current_value: CI runs on Buildkite. PRs need a green build plus 1 review to merge. Flaky tests are quarantined after 3 failures in a week.

messages.text: the orders deploy is stuck again, migrate-bot has been running for 11 minutes
that migration adds an index on a 200M row table, it was never going to fit in 5 min
should we bump max migration duration to 10 min?
band-aid. the real problem is migrations blocking deploys at all
what if migrations ran as their own pipeline step before the deploy instead of during it?
we tried that at my last job, worked well
ok, decision: migrations move to a separate pre-deploy pipeline step, no longer run automatically during deploy. raj can you make the change
on it
morning! did I miss anything fun
only kofi redesigning the deploy pipeline before 9am
merged the pipeline change, migrations are their own step now
on flags — the audit found 74 stale flags. some are two years old
74?? our policy says 90 days
nobody enforces it. I propose we drop the window to 30 days AND have the bot auto-open cleanup PRs
30 feels aggressive, some flags legitimately live for a quarter
those get an extended expiry from their owner, that's what the expiry field is for
discussed with mei offline — decided in the infra sync: stale flag window drops from 90 to 30 days, extensions need owner sign-off
🎉 cleanup PRs start monday
hm, seeing something weird — deploys now take twice as long since migrations are a separate step, the runner spins up twice
ugh. can we share the runner between steps?
not on our buildkite plan
not worth it then. rolling that back — migrations go back to running automatically on deploy like before, raj revert the pipeline change
reverted 🫡
so about my original question, the 5 min cap still bites us on big tables
let's collect data on actual migration durations for a week and revisit
the checkout test failed 3 times today alone. quarantine rules say a week but this thing is clearly broken
propose we quarantine after 2 failures in 48h instead of 3 in a week
sure, let's just YOLO everything straight to prod while we're at it, tests are optional anyway 🙃
ana 😂 I'm serious though
approved 👍
quarantining checkout test now, finally
separate thing — the schema-touching PRs keep sneaking in with a single rubber-stamp review
the payments incident last month was exactly that
options: require 2 reviews on schema PRs, or a mandatory DBA review, or a lint gate on migration files
DBA review means waiting on the one DBA we have
yeah let's go with 2 reviews for schema-touching PRs then, everything else stays at 1
will the branch protection rule handle path-based review counts?
with a CODEOWNERS trick, yes. I'll set it up
unrelated, we're adopting pnpm across the platform repos, npm installs are killing CI time. already agreed with the leads
bless. npm install is 40% of our build time
we'll probably need to revisit the flag expiry policy someday, 30 days will generate noise
someday™
does anyone remember why destructive migrations need a snapshot? we have PITR on all databases now
history. worth questioning but not deciding in a random thread — adding it to the infra sync agenda
the checkout test is green after quarantine + fix btw
week's migration duration data is in: p99 is 4m50s. that 5 min cap is one big table away from biting again
ok, data's convincing — max migration duration goes to 15 minutes, update migrate-bot's timeout
done and done
